creator: n4aof

content:
I believe some or all the Unified Folders indexes are corrupt. Try this:

- Click on <Help | Troubleshooting Information>, go to "Profile Folder", then click the "Open Folder" button. This will open up your system's fil browser in your Profile folder.

- Close down Thunderbird.

- Navigate into the "Mail\smart mailboxes" folder. Delete all files there with the .msf extension. They will be rebuilt as needed next time you run Thunderbird.

- Now restart Thunderbird.


Does that fix the problem? If not, the actual individual folder indexes may be corrupt. The fix for that is to right-click on each "real" folder name in the list, choose Properties from the context menu, then press the "Repair Folder" button. You would have to do this to every suspect folder, which could be tedious, so try to first method first.
creator: rednoise

content: Thank you, Lin, that seems to have solved the problem (as least for now).

content:
Mapenzi said
In the Folder Pane font colour in account names and folder names changes to blue when new messages arrive. It is as simple as that.

That doesn't seem to be what's going on. The third account doesn't have any unread messages. Unless "new" means something other than "unread"?
creator: spartanhooah

content: "New" messages are emails newly downloaded from server when you start Thunderbird or which arrive during working with Thunderbird. They are necessarily unread too until you open them. If you close Thunderbird without reading new messages they will be displayed as unread on next start .
creator: Mapenzi

content: You didn't provide version of thunderbird. I will assume 140. If 150, just highlight account, rightclick and select 'reset folder order'. Another way, which works in 140, is to use the config editor and set mailnews.default_sort_order to a value of 2.

The above solution did not solve the problem. My version is 140.10.1esr (64bit). I will really appreciate a fix.
